title: hieracium bupleuroides subsp. pantotrichum
intro: Hieracium bupleuroides subsp. pantotrichum, also known as a plant species within the dandelion-like hawkweed family, is a type of flowering plant. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as aiding in digestion and reducing fever. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
